completed.title: Pirateland
completed.synopsis: As winter begins on an overly touristic Greek island in Crete, a struggling local family running a small bed & breakfast receive an unexpected visit from Norwegian tourists seeking the authentic pirate experience. The relationship between Manos, the Greek father, and Tassos, his son, is tested as their demands grow ever more challenging.

credits.label: Co-produced by
credits.value: Alaska Films (GR), Yukunkun Productions (FR), Ferdi Film (NOR), Onassis Culture (GR), GFC (GR)

credits.label: Supported by
credits.value: ARTE France, Region of Creta, Indigo View

festivals: Clermont-Ferrand International Film Festival — International Competition — World Premiere

completed.title: Panellinion
completed.synopsis: Panellinion is an out-of-place-and-time chess coffeehouse in the centre of Athens — a refuge for people who suffocate in everyday life. Giannis, the owner, hates chess yet looks upon the regulars as his children: scientists, artists, retirees, workers who seek outlet in chess, make confessions, recall old stories of legendary eccentric customers.
festivals: Thessaloniki International Documentary Festival — Best Newcomer Award, Best Greek Film Award, Youth Jury Special Prize
Iris 2025 — Hellenic Film Academy Awards — Nominated for Best Documentary

completed.title: End of August
completed.synopsis: That summer, Amelia will discover her mother's secret love affair. Refusing to believe what she saw, she runs away from home with Epaminondas, her first love. They will live an adventure, testing themselves and everyone they meet, ending up at the end of the night as children again in the arms of their parents.

credits.label: Co-produced by
credits.value: Alaska Films, Felony Film, Ready2Cast, Rentphotovideo, GFC & ERT

completed.title: Microbiome
completed.synopsis: The limits of intrusion in the peaceful lives of the local folk on the island of Ikaria are tested as scientists quest to find the secret to long and healthy life. A clash of energies between locals and perplexed scientists — a comedic study in pacing and reframing a world view.
festivals: Sarajevo International Film Festival — International Competition

completed.title: Homes
completed.synopsis: Ten site-specific shows and performances examine the traumatic experience of forceful displacement, the violent loss of one's home and the sense of belonging. The film follows artists through Athens and Piraeus creating notional shared spaces on the borderline between fiction and reality.

completed.title: X Apartments
completed.synopsis: Fifteen apartments, two routes in two forgotten neighbourhoods of Athens, two spectators. A documentary about a transmedial group performance and the questions it raises — searching for the boundaries between art and reality, private and public space, voyeurism and intimacy.

completed.title: I am a Dancer
completed.synopsis: Four persons with different disabilities come in contact with contemporary dance in a series of workshops organised by the Onassis Cultural Centre. Under the two-year guidance of Greek choreographers, they experiment with movement, discover their potential and contribute to three pieces for the International Unlimited Access Dance Festival.

in_production.title: Magnifier
in_production.synopsis: Life with someone suffering from Alzheimer's disease resembles the slow-motion crumbling of a building. The sometimes momentary loss of one's face is stretched through time — a gradual denudation where it parts with levels of itself until there's only the core: a naked feeling without any memory. Thekla's documentary places this course in filmic time, having begun filming since the first diagnosis of her mother's disease.

credits.label: Co-produced by
credits.value: Alaska Films (GR), Yukunkun Production (FR), Grand Slam Film (SE)

credits.label: Produced by
